title: Check how we fare against https://github.com/sindresorhus/file-type/tree/main/fixture
author: gabriel-vasile

title: Microsoft Office PPT, PPTX, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX are not detected
author: gabriel-vasile
body:
When PPT, DOC, XLS, as well as PPTX, DOCX and XLSX cannot be detected, try increasing the amount of bytes used for detection:
```go
mimetype.SetLimit(1024*1024) // Set limit to 1MB.
// or
mimetype.SetLimit(0) // No limit, whole file content used.
mimetype.DetectFile("file.doc")
```

The problem happens because `mimetype` reads only the first `4KB` from the file and sometimes the conclusive signatures cannot be found in first `4KB` of data.
Besides the formats listed above, same limitation applies for VSDX, PUB, MSG, MSI, JAR, KMZ, APK.
Notice that `mimetype` loads the bytes used for detection in **one chunk**, so make sure there is enough memory for it.


Previous discussions about this issue: #614, #790, #325, #231, #388.

title: Ttf() does not recognize TTF fonts whose first offset table entry is an OpenType layout table (GDEF, GPOS, GSUB)
author: adriancmiranda
body:
**Attach the file for which the detection is inaccurate**
https://github.com/google/fonts/raw/main/ofl/figtree/Figtree-Italic%5Bwght%5D.ttf

**Expected MIME type**
font/ttf

**Returned MIME type**
application/octet-stream

**Version of the library you are using**
v1.4.13

**Output of `go version`**
go version go1.26.3 darwin/arm64

**Additional context**
Ttf() checks hasSFNTTable() which only inspects raw[12:16] (the first table tag in the offset table) against a hardcoded list of TrueType-specific tags. OpenType layout tables (GDEF=0x47444546, GPOS, GSUB, BASE, JSTF, MATH) are absent from that list, so any font that sorts one of them first is rejected.

Fix: add those tags to possibleTables, or scan all numTables entries instead of only the first.

title: Apng detection error
author: aichinuomifan
body:
**Expected MIME type**
image/vnd.mozilla.apng

**Returned MIME type**
image/png

**Version of the library you are using**
1.4.12

**Output of `go version`**
1.21

**Additional context**
func Apng(raw []byte, _ uint32) bool {
        return offset(raw, []byte("acTL"), 37)
}
In func Apng(...), it is considered that acTL is at byte 37, but in actual apng files, byte 37 is not necessarily acTL; it could be sRGB, PLTE...

title: text/rtf can have a trailing 0x00 byte
author: gabriel-vasile
body:
RTF detection works fine for most samples, but Wordpad and microsoft word sometimes put a 0x00 at the end of the file.


Since RTF is a subtype of text, the 0x00 byte fails the text check and the file is considered `application/octet-stream`.

Options:
- do nothing
- move rtf so it is no longer a subtype of text -> would make rtf the single text/xxx mime that is not actually under text/plain. It would break the [BinaryVSText](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/gabriel-vasile/mimetype#example-package-TextVsBinary) example.
- instruct text check to ignore that single trailing 0x00 and then, somehow, have just the rtf check proceed.

The trailing 0x00 affects other software too:
https://github.com/henck/rtf-html-php/issues/65#issuecomment-571330568
https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/cant-open-rtf-files-in-text-editor/1154782

title: Please export `defaultLimit`
author: pjebs
body:
Currently:
`const defaultLimit uint32 = 3072`.

For my usage case I am setting `SetLimit()` occasionally.
Sometimes I would like to return it back to the original state.

Currently I am hardcoding it to 3072 but you might change it in the future.

title: Size from start and end
author: pjebs
body:
Some mime types have data towards the end that reveal what it's mime type is.
It would be good if we can set how much data to read from the START and END.

Currently we can only set reading from the start OR the entire file.

title: ndjson not recognized when json object is too big
author: alexl-splunk
body:
**Attach the file for which the detection is inaccurate**
You can use the file sharing site of your choice, just ensure the file will not expireor get deleted.

[bigjson.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24218632/bigjson.json)

**Expected MIME type**
application/nd-json

**Returned MIME type**
text/plain; charset=utf-8

**Version of the library you are using**
github.com/gabriel-vasile/mimetype v1.4.8

**Output of `go version`**
go version go1.25.3 darwin/arm64

**Additional context**
context:
- readLimit is set to 3072
- for any ndjson with object that is "big" enough, only 1 json object would be read
- paired with this conditional https://github.com/gabriel-vasile/mimetype/blob/master/internal/magic/text.go#L404, it is guaranteed to not be recognized as ndjson. 

Suggestion:
Change conditional from
`return lCount > 1 && objOrArr > 0`
to
`return lCount > 0 && objOrArr > 0`

What is the rationale to have `lCount > 1`?

Alternatively, you can fork the project, commit the file with a failing test and reference the commit in the issue.

title: Shebang support and whitespace
author: scop
body:
Shebang matching against files that use different amount of whitespace than what is set in mimetype's signatures fails to match. For example, the signature `/usr/bin/env python` (one space) does not match a file with shebang `#!/usr/bin/env  python` (two spaces).

These are kind of corner cases, but perhaps they could be addressed anyway. Possible approaches include at least normalizing whitespace in the shebang to be checked to match how signatures are written (probably to just one literal space between tokens), making signatures regular expressions as opposed to literal strings, or specifying signatures as slices of string tokens and comparing them against whitespace tokenized shebangs.

Of the above, regular expressions aren't that nice, but they would make the matching most flexible and yield support for some more cases that are currently not available. For example, the signature `/usr/bin/python(\d[a-z\d\.]+)?` could be used to capture for example `/usr/bin/python3.13` without having to enumerate all version possibilities.

title: Add support for inode/* MIME types in DetectFile
author: gabriel-vasile
body:
Only for DetectFile, `inode/*` MIME types could be detected.

inode/blockdevice
inode/chardevice
inode/directory
inode/fifo
inode/mount-point
inode/socket
inode/symlink

How to implement: with a call to `stat` before doing actual detection
https://github.com/file/file/blob/master/src/fsmagic.c
